summary: Phase 7f delta tested: 20 pytest cases run (17 passed, 2 failed, 1 skipped). Frontend Admin Console verified end-to-end: /admin login (magic-link fallback, OTP send hit IP rate-limit from repeated test runs), link-admin-console navigates to /admin/console, all 4 tabs (transactions/orders/commissions/audit) render their content panels, commission-summary + commission-total + commission-rate-input + commission-rate-save all present and working (0.075 rate save succeeded). Endpoint count = 176 as expected. Two REAL backend bugs discovered.

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error. Root cause: export_transactions_csv() calls list_transactions(_admin=_admin) directly as a Python function, but FastAPI Query() defaults are NOT resolved to actual values in this direct-call path — they remain fastapi.params.Query objects. Then _parse_dt(from_date) does s.replace('Z','+00:00') on a Query object → AttributeError: 'Query' object has no attribute 'replace'. Fix: extract the shared list_transactions body into a plain helper (e.g. _query_transactions()) that both the endpoint and the CSV export call with actual string/None values.

Trust+ bonus wallet credit is silently FAILING. services.trust_plus_service.maybe_award_bonus calls wallet_service.credit(...) but services.wallet_service has NO 'credit' function (it has earn_credits / spend_credits / deposit_inr / purchase_inr). Backend logs: `services.trust_plus_service - WARNING - wallet credit failed: module 'services.wallet_service' has no attribute 'credit'`. Because it's swallowed by a bare except in trust_plus_service, the user.has_received_trusted_plus_bonus flag STILL flips to True + is_trusted_plus:True → users are permanently marked as bonus-awarded but never actually receive the 100 credits. Idempotency guarantee still holds but the bonus itself never lands. Fix: call wallet_service.earn_credits(user_id, BONUS_CREDITS, reason='trust_plus_bonus', ref_type='kyc', ref_id=f'trust_plus:{user_id}').

minor.issue: amount_paise for wallet transactions is calculated as `(w.get('amount_credits') or 0) * 100` but wallet_transactions collection stores the field as `amount` (int credits), not `amount_credits`. All wallet rows show ₹0 in the console UI. Should be `w.get('amount') or w.get('amount_credits') or 0`.

integration_issues.issue: download-csv is a <button> (not an <a href=...>). Clicking it hits GET /admin/transactions.csv which returns 500 (see backend bug #1). Once backend is fixed, verify the button either navigates via window.open or downloads via blob.

action_items: FIX Trust+ bonus: replace wallet_service.credit(...) call with wallet_service.earn_credits(...) in /app/backend/services/trust_plus_service.py:maybe_award_bonus. Currently no user actually receives the +100 credits.
FIX CSV export: /app/backend/routes/admin_plus_routes.py:export_transactions_csv — cannot call list_transactions() directly with Query() defaults. Extract a plain helper or pass explicit None values.
FIX wallet_transactions field mismatch in admin_plus_routes.py:list_transactions — read `amount` (existing schema), not `amount_credits`, so amount_paise reflects real credit movement.
critical_code_review_comments: trust_plus_service.maybe_award_bonus wraps wallet credit in `try/except Exception:` that only logs a warning — even when the credit fails hard (missing attribute), the user's has_received_trusted_plus_bonus flag is still set to True. This means once the bug is fixed, ALREADY-flipped users will never get their retroactive +100 credits without a backfill script.

admin_plus_routes.py:export_transactions_csv calls the sibling endpoint function directly as `list_transactions(_admin=_admin)`. This is an anti-pattern in FastAPI — Query()/Depends() defaults are only resolved when FastAPI is the caller. Direct calls need explicit args.

Cart guard: services.identity_service.has_verified_identity is invoked per vendor in the checkout loop (N+1 queries). For carts with 5+ vendors this is noticeable; consider one aggregate lookup over kyc_documents.

Commission serializer returns `None` when input is falsy but the type hint is `dict`. If list_commissions ever includes a soft-deleted or malformed row, `[_serialize(c) for c in docs]` could inject None into the list.
identity_routes.py: format-validate-BEFORE-rate-limit order is now correct (verified: 6 invalid + 1 valid within same window all pass through).
admin_plus_routes.py CSV response would leak arbitrary CSV injection if id/reference fields ever contained '='/'+'/'-'/'@' prefixes; consider prefixing with a single quote on export.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin console + all tabs + commission-rate-save verified end-to-end)

test_credentials: Admin phone 9039791530 (dev_otp echoed, but OTP send has IP rate-limit that trips after repeated pytest runs — use magic link fallback: /admin/login?token=j0XOw7fmnW8-WDX59eZP4dkIQyxmGS5g7yroYUGKRRnXt-_3qQZtiQ8AyK2otlqN).
seed_data_creation: Fresh random-phone customer + vendor users per pytest run. Global commission rate set to 0.075 (last save from UI); global was previously set to 0.06 by pytest. One test category_id='test_cat_abc' has rate 0.10.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two real bugs need main-agent fix: (1) wallet_service.credit missing → Trust+ bonus never credits; (2) CSV export 500 due to Query default not resolved. After fixes: re-run test_phase7f.py and specifically re-verify TestTrustPlus::test_second_doc_awards_bonus and TestAdminConsole::test_transactions_csv. Also check that already-flipped is_trusted_plus users get retroactive credit (may need backfill script). Cart-guard test SKIPPED because listing creation payload didn't match (schema likely requires different fields); the empty-cart 400 path is verified but the actual vendor_unverified 403 path was only verified manually via curl per E1's note.
rca_of_the_issue: Bug 1 (Trust+ bonus): trust_plus_service.py:56 calls `wallet_service.credit(...)` but wallet_service only exposes earn_credits/spend_credits/deposit_inr/purchase_inr. The generic try/except in trust_plus_service swallowed the AttributeError, so all downstream flag updates (`has_received_trusted_plus_bonus=True`, `is_trusted_plus=True`) still happened — creating a phantom-awarded state. Reproduction: log in as fresh user → verify 1st doc (aadhaar 123456789012) → verify 2nd doc (pan ABCDE1234F) → GET /kyc/trust-plus/me shows bonus_awarded:true; GET /wallet/me shows unchanged balance. Fix: replace `wallet_service.credit(user_id, BONUS_CREDITS, source=..., ref_id=...)` with `wallet_service.earn_credits(user_id, BONUS_CREDITS, reason='trust_plus_bonus', ref_type='kyc', ref_id=f'trust_plus:{user_id}')`. Bug 2 (CSV 500): admin_plus_routes.py:101 does `data = await list_transactions(_admin=_admin)` — a direct Python call bypasses FastAPI's dependency-injection so unset params keep their `fastapi.params.Query(...)` sentinel objects. Then _parse_dt tries `.replace()` on that object → AttributeError. Reproduction: GET /api/v1/admin/transactions.csv with valid admin JWT → 500. Fix: extract shared listing logic into `async def _fetch_transactions(...):` taking plain typed args (`from_date: str|None = None`), and have both the CSV endpoint AND list_transactions call it.
